prompt: A medium-shot authentic daily life photograph, natural candid moment, of an East Asian young woman standing at a kitchen window on a rain-grey afternoon. 85mm lens, waist-up framing, shallow depth of field. Behind her: dark wooden kitchen surfaces, a single porcelain bowl upturned to dry, the window glass blurred with rain-diffused light outside. East Asian young woman in her early 20s. Almond-shaped eyes looking toward the window, quiet and unfocused, the expression of someone watching rain without expectation. Delicate straight nose. Skin tone fair beige, the overcast light removing all shadow, flattening her complexion to something almost porcelain. Skin subsurface scattering visible under directional light, specular micro-highlights on cheekbones and nose ridge, fine foundation powder grain perceptible. She wears a moss green silk qipao with bamboo-stalk embroidery in tones of grey-green and dark earth — the pattern modest and close to the fabric's own color, the green carrying the weight of old growth. Mandarin collar, short sleeves. She stands at the window ledge with both hands resting on the sill, looking out at the grey. An overturned bowl, a folded cloth — nothing more. Two or three stray hairs displaced by faint air movement, natural unplanned imperfection, not geometrically symmetrical. Rainy overcast light — flat, cool, shadowless — floods the kitchen without warmth. The moss green absorbs it like lichen absorbs shade. No contrast, no drama. Mood: the particular peace of rain as permission to do nothing. Subtle ISO 400 film grain in shadow areas, photographic noise texture not CG render smoothness. Aspect ratio 9:16 . No watermark, no text overlay, not cartoon, not digitally painted, not illustration, not anime.
